judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent loaded 2019 grant records from publication_database (var_function-call-16775230555930352805) and, in its final processing (function-call-5566358083024197037), computed EMAs but never queried CPCDefinition_database to map CPC codes to level-4 groups or retrieve titleFull, instead slicing the first four characters of CPC codes and using patent titles from title_localized, leading to wrong CPC group codes and titles versus the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final contributing execute_python step (processing var_function-call-15167329076479841775), the agent filtered for Germany by regex-matching the literal '"country_code": "DE"' in the natural-language Patents_info field, causing zero matches and an empty result set, and thus incorrectly returned "No CPC…" instead of computing EMAs and joining CPC titles.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final contributing step (execute_python id function-call-4381798558824695840), the agent filtered CPC definitions with cpc_definitions_df['level'] == 4.0 while the 'level' values from var_function-call-2445608865627351311 are strings like "4.0", resulting in an empty level-4 symbol set and thus no CPC groups matched, leading to an empty result and the incorrect conclusion.

B. Failure modes encountered:

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final execute_python step (function-call-8704170137434887466), the agent computed EMA by grouping CPC codes using the first four characters (e.g., B60K, B60R) and then mapped each to the 3-character level-4 symbols (e.g., B60) without consolidating, resulting in duplicate entries like multiple B60 rows with different best years instead of a single per-level-4 CPC group result.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed EMAs and best years at the full CPC code level (execute_python function-call-14768448611898660171) and then simply mapped codes to level‑4 prefixes without aggregating or selecting the highest EMA per level‑4 group (execute_python function-call-10475895854409059404), resulting in duplicate groups (e.g., B60 twice) and not identifying the true highest EMA groups required by the query.
